draft.title: a CRM for freelancers
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
a CRM for freelancers

Building your first draft…

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer interfaces that feel approachable and friendly rather than corporate and austere, and respond better to curved UI elements with gentle shadows
facade.label: Soft rounded cards
facade.acceptImplies: Explore rounded corners, soft shadows, and welcoming color palettes for the core CRM interface
facade.rejectImplies: Consider sharp edges, high contrast, and geometric precision as potential design directions

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers respond better to interfaces with warm color accents and contained navigation—creating a sense of coziness and control in their workspace
facade.label: Warm sidebar
facade.acceptImplies: User gravitates toward intimate, personal workspace aesthetics; likely values comfort and approachability in tool design
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer cooler, more professional or minimal aesthetics; possibly values clarity and distance over warmth

evidence.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer interfaces that feel approachable and friendly rather than corporate and austere, and respond better to curved UI elements with gentle shadows
evidence.implication: Explore rounded corners, soft shadows, and welcoming color palettes for the core CRM interface

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers respond better to friendly, conversational voice over corporate formality — they want a CRM that feels like a helpful friend, not enterprise software
facade.label: Warm, chatty tone
facade.acceptImplies: Design copy, UI microcopy, and error states with warmth, humor, and personality. Use contractions. Celebrate small wins. Avoid jargon.
facade.rejectImplies: Lean into professional, neutral, business-formal tone. Minimize personality. Use complete sentences. Prioritize clarity and authority over warmth.

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer scannable card layouts over dense tables for browsing their client roster
facade.label: Client list as cards
facade.acceptImplies: Visual card-based design for client views; prioritize at-a-glance status, last contact, next deadline
facade.rejectImplies: Spreadsheet-style tables and full-data rows are acceptable; users want comprehensive inline information over visual hierarchy

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer a persistent navigation sidebar (showing clients, projects, invoices) paired with a card-based content area for quick visual scanning of their active work
facade.axisTargeted: Navigation architecture (sidebar vs tabs) combined with content density (cards vs lists)
facade.label: Sidebar with cards
facade.acceptImplies: User gravitates toward hierarchical, always-visible navigation with grouped visual blocks—suggests preference for persistent context and chunked information over linear tabs and dense text
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer tab-based navigation, list views, or a flatter structure—suggests they want focused attention on one section at a time or need to scan more information at once without visual break

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prioritize speed over ceremony—they want to tap a name and see full details immediately, no slide-out or modal delay
facade.axisTargeted: Transition speed and directness of information access
facade.label: Instant contact open
facade.acceptImplies: The CRM should favor instant-reveal patterns: instant search, instant detail views, minimal loading states. Gesture-heavy, low latency.
facade.rejectImplies: The CRM should use considered reveals: modals, slide-outs, staged information. Animation-first approach that values transition moments over pure speed.

axes.label: Whether they prefer minimal, clean aesthetics vs. rich visual hierarchy with color/imagery—reveals if they want a distraction-free workspace or one that feels energetic and branded
Whether they favor dense information dashboards vs. progressive disclosure—reveals if they want everything visible at once or prefer guided, step-by-step navigation
Whether they respond to professional/formal tone vs. casual/supportive tone—reveals if they want business-like efficiency or human encouragement
Whether they prefer calm, minimal atmosphere vs. energetic, motivational atmosphere—reveals if they want zen focus or inspiring momentum
Whether they want client/project data front-and-center vs. financial/metrics focus—reveals what information matters most to their freelance workflow
Whether they prefer instant feedback and snappy interactions vs. smooth, considered animations—reveals if they want speed and directness or polish and delight

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they prefer minimal, clean aesthetics vs. rich visual hierarchy with color/imagery—reveals if they want a distraction-free workspace or one that feels energetic and branded
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they favor dense information dashboards vs. progressive disclosure—reveals if they want everything visible at once or prefer guided, step-by-step navigation
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they respond to professional/formal tone vs. casual/supportive tone—reveals if they want business-like efficiency or human encouragement
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they prefer calm, minimal atmosphere vs. energetic, motivational atmosphere—reveals if they want zen focus or inspiring momentum
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they want client/project data front-and-center vs. financial/metrics focus—reveals what information matters most to their freelance workflow
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they prefer instant feedback and snappy interactions vs. smooth, considered animations—reveals if they want speed and directness or polish and delight
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers working across multiple client projects may prefer a restrained, single-color interface that reduces visual noise and cognitive load, allowing content and data to take center stage without distraction
facade.axisTargeted: Color complexity and visual density — exploring whether freelancers lean toward rich, warm palettes or stripped-down, austere aesthetics
facade.label: Monochrome minimalist
facade.acceptImplies: Pursue desaturated, single-hue or grayscale design systems; prioritize whitespace and content clarity over decorative color; test neutral sidebar and accent-only UI patterns
facade.rejectImplies: Freelancers likely need visual variety and tonal contrast to navigate multiple projects and clients; explore richer color palettes, subtle gradients, or multi-tone systems that add visual hierarchy without feeling corporate
